title: Generate All Subsets — O(2ⁿ)
description:
Given a set of DISTINCT integers, return all possible subsets (the power set). The empty set and the full set are both included.

**The point**: this is genuinely O(2ⁿ) — there are 2ⁿ subsets so you can't do better than enumerate them. This problem shows what 'exponential' looks like in friendly form.

**Output format**: return subsets in the iteration order produced by the canonical recursion (see hints). The grader checks for an exact-order match.
hints: There are 2ⁿ subsets — one for each subset of indices. So O(2ⁿ) is unavoidable.
Backtracking: at each step, decide whether to include the next element. After recursing, undo the choice.
Push a snapshot of the running subset at the START of each call — that's how the empty set gets included.

testCases.description: empty input has one subset: the empty set
